questions.question: What is the topic for Week 1?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly provided for Lesson One is THEOS BASILEIA.

questions.question: What is the Greek word for 'God' used in the lesson title?
questions.explanation: The introduction states that the Greek word 'Theos' means God.

questions.question: How is the Greek word 'Basileia' translated according to the lesson?
options: B. Kingdom, realm, rule and royalty
questions.explanation: The lesson introduces 'Basileia' as translating to kingdom, realm, rule and royalty.

questions.question: What does the full term 'Theos Basileia' refer to?
questions.explanation: Theos Basileia refers directly to the Kingdom of God.

questions.question: The concept of the kingdom of God is generally considered to be the central theme of the teaching of Jesus.
questions.explanation: The introduction states this concept is generally considered the central theme of Jesus' teaching.

questions.question: According to Matthew 19:23-24 referenced in the lesson, the Kingdom of God is also known as what?
questions.explanation: The lesson explicitly notes that the kingdom is also known as the kingdom of heaven.

questions.question: But seek first the kingdom of God and His ___________, and all these things shall be added to you.
questions.explanation: The memory verse is Matthew 6:33 which says 'and His righteousness'.

questions.question: In which book, chapter, and verse is the memory verse for this week found? (Use format: Book Chapter:Verse)
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Matthew 6:33.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 1 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things shall be added to you.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Matthew 6:33.

questions.question: What is the primary command given in this week's memory verse?
options: B. To seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness
C. To love the Lord with all your heart
D. To bless the Lord before all the assembly
questions.explanation: The memory verse commands believers to seek the kingdom of God first.

questions.question: What is the exact heading for Outline I in this lesson?
questions.answer: ASPECTS OF THE KINGDOM OF GOD
questions.explanation: The first main teaching outline is headed 'ASPECTS OF THE KINGDOM OF GOD'.

questions.question: How many aspects of God's kingdom are listed in Outline I?
questions.explanation: The lesson lists three aspects: Universal, Mediatorial, and Spiritual.

questions.question: Which aspect of God's kingdom is defined as God's eternal sovereign rule over all that exists?
questions.explanation: The Universal Kingdom is described as God's eternal sovereign rule over the entire universe.

questions.question: Daniel 4:3 is used in the lesson to support the concept of the Spiritual Kingdom.
questions.explanation: Daniel 4:3 is used to support the Universal Kingdom, not the Spiritual Kingdom.

questions.question: In the Mediatorial Kingdom, who does God use to speak on His behalf?
questions.explanation: The text states God uses divinely chosen human representatives to speak on His behalf.

questions.question: According to Outline I, the Mediatorial Kingdom will end with what major event?
options: A. The rapture of the church
C. The thousand-year (millennial) reign of Christ upon the earth
D. The new heaven and new earth
questions.explanation: The text says 'The Mediatorial Kingdom will end with the thousand-year (millennial) reign of Christ upon the earth'.

questions.question: What is the concluding link between human history and the eternal kingdom according to Revelation 21-22 mentioned in the lesson?
options: B. The Mediatorial Kingdom's end in the millennial reign
questions.explanation: The millennial reign ending the Mediatorial Kingdom is the concluding link to the eternal kingdom.

questions.question: Which kingdom is described as an invisible kingdom that exists in the hearts of believers?
questions.explanation: The Spiritual Kingdom is an invisible kingdom in the hearts of believers.

questions.question: In John 18:36, Jesus said His kingdom was indeed of this physical world.
questions.explanation: The text says 'Jesus said His kingdom was not of this world (John 18:36)'.

questions.question: According to Matthew 4:17 referenced in Outline I, what did Jesus preach is necessary to be a part of the kingdom of God?
questions.explanation: He preached that repentance is necessary to be a part of the kingdom of God.

questions.question: In the Bible Passage (1 Chronicles 29:10-12), who is the character that blessed the Lord before all the assembly?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'Therefore David blessed the Lord before all the assembly'.

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Yours, O Lord, is the greatness, The power and the glory, The victory and the majesty; For all that is in heaven and in earth is Yours...'
questions.verse_text: Yours, O Lord, is the greatness, The power and the glory, The victory and the majesty; For all that is in heaven and in earth is Yours...
questions.explanation: This is part of the main Bible passage for the week, 1 Chronicles 29:11.

questions.question: What is the exact heading for Outline II in this lesson?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'SEEKING THE KINGDOM OF GOD'.

questions.question: In what famous teaching does Jesus admonish His audience to first seek the Kingdom of God?
options: C. The Sermon on the Mount
D. The Parable of the Sower
questions.explanation: The text specifies: 'In His Sermon on the Mount, Jesus admonishes His audience...'

questions.question: According to Outline II, seeking the kingdom of God means to hunger and thirst for the rule and reign of God with whom being in charge?
questions.explanation: The text says 'with Jesus being in charge'.

questions.question: What does the lesson state is the MAIN benefit of the kingdom (supported by Acts 3:19)?
questions.explanation: The text says 'The main benefit of the kingdom is the forgiveness of sins (Acts 3:19).'

questions.question: The kingdom of God is not rules and regulations but righteousness and peace and joy in the ________.
questions.explanation: Quoting Romans 14:17, it is 'righteousness and peace and joy in the Holy Spirit'.

questions.question: The kingdom of God can be equated with the sphere of salvation that must be entered into by being ________.
questions.explanation: The text says it must be entered into by being born again (John 3:5-7).

questions.question: According to the lesson, 1 Corinthians 6:9-10 is used to explain the Universal Kingdom.
questions.explanation: 1 Corinthians 6:9-10 is referenced in the section on the Spiritual Kingdom, regarding entering the sphere of salvation.

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Both riches and honor come from You, And You reign over all. In Your hand is power and might...'
questions.verse_text: Both riches and honor come from You, And You reign over all. In Your hand is power and might...
questions.explanation: This is verse 12 of the main Bible passage 1 Chronicles 29:10-12.

questions.question: Which set of scriptures does the lesson use to prove that the Spiritual kingdom exists in the hearts of believers?
questions.explanation: The lesson says 'exists in the hearts of believers in Christ Jesus (Luke 17:20-21)'.

questions.question: Which Bible reference is given in the Conclusion to support witnessing to others that the kingdom is open to all?
questions.explanation: The conclusion cites Mark 16:15-16.

questions.question: In the Universal Kingdom, God shares His absolute sovereignty with the angels (Psalm 47:2).
questions.explanation: The lesson explicitly states 'God rules in absolute sovereignty over all His creation'.

questions.question: Seeking the kingdom of God requires us to do what regarding His will?
options: B. Submit totally and obey it
C. Teach it to others only
questions.explanation: Outline II says 'We must also be willing to submit totally and obey His will'.

questions.question: Which reference is associated with the statement that the Spiritual kingdom is 'the fulfilment on earth of God’s will'?
questions.explanation: The text says 'or the fulfilment on earth of God’s will (Matthew 6:10).'

questions.question: The concept of the kingdom of God takes on only one single meaning across all passages of Scripture.
questions.explanation: The introduction states it takes on 'various shades of meaning in different passages'.

questions.question: According to the lesson, the millennial reign of Christ will last for a thousand years. Which scripture is cited to back this up?
questions.explanation: The millennial reign is supported by Revelation 20:4-6.

questions.question: Who is the kingdom of God open to, according to the conclusion?
options: B. All who will surrender their lives to Jesus
C. Only those who have never sinned
questions.explanation: The conclusion states the kingdom is open 'to all who will surrender their lives to Jesus'.

questions.question: What is the topic of Week 1?
options: C. BAPTISM OF THE HOLY SPIRIT
questions.explanation: The topic of Week 1 is THEOS BASILEIA. (Note: other options will be populated with actual topics in a full multi-week run).

questions.question: In the Universal Kingdom, God rules in absolute sovereignty over what?
questions.explanation: The lesson says 'God rules in absolute sovereignty over all His creation'.
